name: Easter Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Obtain a leg of lamb from a butcher and request that they bone it out and butterfly it.
2. Do not have the butcher tie the meat; instead, use butcher string to secure it yourself at home.
3. If the butcher did not remove all the fat inside during processing, do so manually.
4. Lay the boneless lamb flat on a clean surface, skin side down.
5. Remove and discard the fuzzy top of the fennel bulb and wash the remaining stalks thoroughly.
6. Cut the fennel into 2-inch (4 cm) pieces using everything; save the bulb for serving.
7. Season the meat generously with salt and pepper.
8. Spread garlic evenly over the meat, then arrange the fennel pieces on top.
9. Roll the meat tightly like a jellyroll, tying it every 2 inches (5 cm) to secure.
10. Place the tied roast in a roasting pan, lightly oiling the top and sides.
11. Season the roast with additional salt and pepper before baking.
12. Cut the fennel bulb into chunks and drizzle with about 2 tablespoons of melted butter or olive oil; bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 1 hour, turning every half-hour.
13. Remove the cover and continue baking uncovered for another hour.
14. Before the last 30 minutes of cooking, add half a cup of white wine to the pan.
15. Once cooked, transfer the meat to a warm platter; avoid cutting too early as it will cool quickly.
16. Return the roasting pan to the stove and make gravy with the drippings, adding flour and water or additional wine/broth as desired. Adjust seasoning, strain if necessary, and serve alongside mashed potatoes or on the side.
